metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_162
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig 2: SV2A/B deficiency causes a decrease in evoked responses induced by isolated action potentials without a change in mini frequency, mini amplitude, apparent Ca2+ sensitivity of release, or the size of the RRP. Fig3: no difference between synapses lacking only SV2B or both SV2A and SV2B in the size of the ionomycin-releasable RRP or the kinetics of ionomycin-induced release. Hence, SV2 acts at a novel step in synaptic vesicle exocytosis that renders primed vesicles Ca2+ responsive.

Comment bij expert: "Conclusions reached from SV2A/B DKO neurons. SV2B KO neurons do not show phenotype (Suppl Fig 1 and Janz et al 1999)."

13/2/2017 Pim
-This paper builds on top of the known presynaptic localization of SV2A/SV2B and its interaction with proteins of the the SNARE-complex that directly executes the SV fusion (see Introduction)
-This paper details the effect of SV2A/SV2B deletion on SV fusion --> priming of the SNARE-complex
-The effect of SV2A is derived from comparisons of SV2B and SV2A/B double KO animals; any difference between these two strains is caused by function of SV2A (see comment expert above). Experimental description: Cultured cortical neurons from SV2A wild-type/SV2B homozygous [SV2A(+/+)/SV2B(-/-)] pups and SV2A/SV2B homozygous [SV2A(-/-)/SV2B(-/-)] pups were used in the experiments in this study
